clubs.name: Manchester City
clubs.history: City rebuilt smartly after a difficult 2024/25. De Bruyne departed but Marmoush, Cherki and Reijnders arrived, giving Guardiola fresh creative options. Haaland and Foden remain the attacking spine of one of Europe's most expensively assembled squads.

clubs.name: Liverpool
clubs.history: Under Arne Slot, Liverpool finished fifth in 2025/26 after a difficult transitional season. Salah remains the talisman but questions surround the squad's depth and consistency heading into the new campaign.
expectations: Challenge for the Premier League title

clubs.name: Arsenal
clubs.history: Arsenal are the 2025/26 Premier League champions — their first title since 2004. Arteta's side powered to 85 points with Saka, Odegaard and Rice leading the way. A golden generation has finally delivered.

clubs.name: Manchester United
clubs.history: Ruben Amorim's United secured Champions League football finishing third in 2025/26. A new 3-4-3 system suits Mainoo and Garnacho. Rashford has left on loan but Hojlund and Diallo offer exciting attacking options.

clubs.name: Chelsea
clubs.history: Enzo Maresca's Chelsea are built around the extraordinary Cole Palmer, who became the Premier League's most creative force in 2025/26. Young talent like Estevao Willian and Levi Colwill are thriving around him.

clubs.name: Tottenham
clubs.history: A difficult 2025/26 for Spurs under Ange Postecoglou. Son remains the captain and leader, with Van de Ven and Romero a top-class defensive partnership. But consistency has been their Achilles heel.

clubs.name: Brighton
clubs.history: Brighton under Fabian Hurzeler continued their remarkable rise, finishing seventh in 2025/26. Mitoma remains their standout talent, with Carlos Baleba emerging as one of the most exciting young midfielders in the division.

clubs.name: Aston Villa
clubs.history: Villa finished fourth in 2025/26 to claim Champions League football for a second consecutive season. Unai Emery's meticulous preparation and Ollie Watkins' 20+ goals were the key ingredients.
expectations: Ollie Watkins to lead the line

clubs.name: Real Madrid
clubs.history: A trophyless 2025/26 for Madrid despite Mbappe, Vinicius and Bellingham. Xabi Alonso was sacked mid-season. Arbeloa now leads a squad that added Trent Alexander-Arnold and Dean Huijsen but faces a summer rebuild.

clubs.name: Barcelona
clubs.history: Barcelona are La Liga champions in 2025/26. Lamine Yamal at 18 is already a global superstar, Raphinha won the Golden Boot and Hansi Flick has restored the club's attacking identity with breathtaking football.
expectations: Yamal to lead the next generation

clubs.name: Atletico Madrid
clubs.history: Simeone's Atletico reached the Champions League semi-finals in 2025/26, losing to Arsenal. Julian Alvarez bagged 18 goals and 9 assists in an outstanding first season. The Argentine striker is now one of the world's best.

clubs.name: Bayern Munich
clubs.history: Vincent Kompany's Bayern won the Bundesliga in 2025/26 with Musiala and Olise forming one of Europe's most electric partnerships. Harry Kane top-scored once again. A Champions League title remains the ultimate ambition.
expectations: Kane and Musiala to fire them to glory

clubs.name: Bayer Leverkusen
clubs.history: Xabi Alonso left for Real Madrid after winning the title the previous season. Leverkusen have adjusted, with Wirtz still the heartbeat of a talented squad. Grimaldo, Boniface and Frimpong ensure they remain dangerous.

clubs.name: AC Milan
clubs.history: Paulo Fonseca's Milan are building around Leao's electric wing play and Maignan's world-class goalkeeping. Pulisic and Reijnders add creativity in midfield. A Serie A title challenge is the clear objective.
expectations: Leao to deliver a consistent season

clubs.name: Inter Milan
clubs.history: Simone Inzaghi's Inter are Serie A's dominant force. Lautaro, Barella and Bastoni form the spine of one of Europe's most cohesive teams. They reached the Champions League final in 2025/26, losing to Arsenal on penalties.
expectations: Lautaro to lead from the front

clubs.name: PSG
clubs.history: Post-Mbappe PSG under Luis Enrique have rediscovered their collective identity. Dembele, Barcola and Donnarumma are world class. Joao Neves and Vitinha run the midfield, with Goncalo Ramos leading the line.

clubs.name: Galatasaray
clubs.history: Victor Osimhen's loan from Napoli transformed Galatasaray's attack. The Nigerian striker has been unstoppable in the Super Lig, making Galatasaray the dominant force in Turkish football once again.

clubs.name: Besiktas
clubs.history: Besiktas are rebuilding with a mix of veterans and emerging Turkish talent. Ersin Destanoglu is one of Turkey's brightest goalkeeping prospects. The fierce Vodafone Park atmosphere remains their greatest weapon.
